verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multipass transmembrane protein, but its residency is on autophagosomal/Golgi/endosomal membranes where it functions as a lipid scramblase during autophagosome biogenesis. It cycles among endomembrane compartments (TGN, endosomes, phagophore) — not the plasma membrane. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display of ATG9A specifically; no germline/developmental narrow-lineage surface display; not a lysosomal TM protein subject to lysosomal exocytosis to PM (resides upstream in TGN/endosomes); no documented PM minority pool or surface biotinylation evidence; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering on the cell exterior. No surface-directed therapeutic programs.
